meaning: n.(单复数同形)儿女,子孙,后代,产物
examples: The offspring contain a mixture of the genetic blueprint of each parent.
Eleanor was now less anxious about her offspring than she had once been.
The comet is going to come back in 2061 and we are all going to be able to see it. Well, our offspring are, anyway.
Bruno and Michel, his half-brother, are the unloved, 40-ish offspring of a promiscuous mother.
It also gives birth to one offspring at a time, rather than a litter.
